title: Smoky Middle-Eastern Rice Pilaf
description: A smoky, aerated, and energizing Middle-Eastern dish featuring whole grains, a sweet and sticky lacquer glaze, and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients. This recipe is perfect for a quick and easy dinner or lunch.

ingredients.name: Basmati rice
ingredients.notes: Brown rice can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked salt can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Avocado oil can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Red onion or sweet onion can be used.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced or crushed garlic can be used.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper can be used.

ingredients.name: Lacquer glaze (maple syrup and water mixture)
ingredients.notes: Optional, sweetener can be adjusted to taste.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped fresh parsley can be used as garnish.

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lemon juice can be used.

ingredients.name: Pistachios
ingredients.notes: Chopped pistachios can be used as garnish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Flaky sea salt can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Vegetable broth can be used as a substitute.

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Avocado oil can be used as a substitute.

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rice and stir to coat with the spice mixture, about 2-3 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
In a small saucepan, combine the maple syrup and water to make the lacquer glaze. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly, until the syrup dissolves.
Stir in the lemon juice and pistachios.
Once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the lacquer glaze, pistachios, and chopped parsley.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and pistachios if desired.
